DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS ROBERT WILLIAMS, ROBERT FLANAGAN, AND BLOOMINGDALE'S INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFFS SUBPOENAS TO SAKS FIFTH AVENUE CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS IS GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS GRANTED IN FULL AS TO MARSHA SANDERS, AS SHE IS NOT THE SUBJECT OF A NEGLIGENT HIRING CLAIM, NOR WAS THERE WAS A SUFFICIENT FACTUAL SHOWING OF THE NEXUS BETWEEN HER FORMER EMPLOYMENT AND THE INSTANT MATTER. IT IS FURTHER GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO DOCUMENT REQUEST NOS: 1, 2, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12 AS TO DEFENDANTS WILLIAMS AND FLANAGAN. IT IS DENIED TO THOSE REMAINING DOCUMENT REQUESTS WHICH PERTAIN TO PREVIOUS INSTANCES, IF ANY, OF WRONGFUL CONDUCT WHICH ARE DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO THE INSTANT DISPUTES, INCLUDING TO THE NEGLIGENT HIRING AND RETENTION CLAIMS. WHILE DEFENDANTS ARE CORRECT THAT DISCOVERY INTO PRIVATE PERSONNEL DOCUMENTS SHOULD BE PURSUED IN THE LEAST INTRUSIVE MANNER, THIS DOES NOT MEAN PLAINTIFF IS LIMITED TO THE TESTIMONY OF THE DEFENDANTS AND WITNESS(ES) WITHOUT THE OPPORTUNITY TO TEST OR REFUTE THAT TESTIMONY. WHILE PREVIOUS INSTANCES OF MISCONDUCT WOULD BE RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE ENTIRE PERSONNEL FILE IS DISCOVERABLE HERE. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoenas To Saks Fifth Avenue Custodian Of Records
Off Calendar Jan-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Hannah Stitt, esq. (415-465-6272) of Ad Astra LLP for Plaintiff Alessandro Garcia.
Cheryl A. Stevens, esq. (415-357-4600) of Lafayette & Kumagai for Defendants ROBERT WILLIAMS, ROBERT FLANAGAN, and BLOOMINGDALE'S INC.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs ROBERT WILLIAMS, ROBERT FLANAGAN, and BLOOMINGDALE'S INC.'s Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoenas To Saks Fifth Avenue Custodian Of Records.

Defendants' Motion to Quash is GRANTED in part, and DENIED in part. Defendants' Motion is GRANTED in full as to Marsha Sanders, as she is not the subject of a negligent hiring claim, nor was there was a sufficient factual showing of the nexus between her former employment and the instant matter. It is further GRANTED with respect to Document Request Nos: 1, 2, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12 as to Defendants Williams and Flanagan. It is DENIED to those remaining document requests which pertain to previous instances, if any, of wrongful conduct which are directly relevant to the instant disputes, including to the negligent hiring and retention claims. While Defendants are correct that discovery into private personnel documents should be pursued in the least intrusive manner, this does not mean Plaintiff is limited to the testimony of the defendants and witness(es) without the opportunity to test or refute that testimony. While previous instances of misconduct would be relevant to Plaintiff's claims, there is no showing that the entire personnel file is discoverable here.
